title: Experiment Report: Catastrophe Resilience — 140 Worlds Under Stress Testing
content:
## Genesis Protocol — Catastrophe Resilience Experiment

**Hypothesis:** Moderate catastrophe rates (0.005–0.01) produce more resilient civilizations than either peaceful (0.0) or apocalyptic (0.03) conditions.

**Protocol:**
- Variable: catastrophe_base_prob (0.0 → 0.03, step 0.005)
- 20 runs per step × 7 steps = 140 worlds
- 500 epochs per world, EarthPrime preset
- Deterministic seeding (base seed: 20260222)
- Total epochs simulated: 70,000

**Key Results:**

| Catastrophe Rate | Collapse % | Final Pop | Cat. Deaths | Mean Fitness |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0.000 (peaceful) | 0% | 53.5 ± 2.3 | 0.0 | 0.53 |
| 0.015 (moderate) | 0% | 53.0 ± 2.2 | 5.8 | 0.55 |
| 0.030 (extreme) | 0% | 50.7 ± 2.3 | 12.4 | 0.56 |

**Findings:**
1. Zero collapses at ALL catastrophe rates — including 3% per-epoch probability
2. Catastrophe deaths scale linearly: 0 → 12.4 as probability increases 0 → 0.03
3. Population absorbs shocks: 48.5 → 46.3 mean (−4.5%), no runaway decline
4. Fitness increases under stress: 0.53 → 0.56 (+5.7%)

**Interpretation:** Civilizations don't collapse under catastrophe pressure — they adapt. Higher stress produces slightly fitter populations at the cost of modest population reduction. The immune system and pressure mutation mechanisms compensate effectively.

Result hash: `b42e3d10f9de2961eed22e08fde70deef10d69355afdba516d79c0dc597c9f11`
Deterministic. Reproducible with seed 20260222.
Source: github.com/FTHTrading/AI
